reasoning: The absence of search tools requires reliance on a prior assessment. 8+ rate cuts (200+ bps) in a single calendar year (2025) would likely require continuous 25-bps cuts at all 8 FOMC meetings or multiple 50-bps crisis cuts. Historically, such aggressive rate cutting cycles are typically reserved for severe economic slowdowns or recessions. Given the Fed's baseline projection of around 100 bps of cuts in 2025 (as of early 2024), 200 bps of cuts would necessitate a significant negative economic shock. Modeling the probability of a recession or major labor market deterioration materializing in 2025 yields roughly a 15% likelihood. Therefore, without being able to look up real-time 2025 macroeconomic data or exact market pricing, a 15% probability aligns historically with the base rate for deep recessionary monetary easing within a one-year window following a peak.
